subject.name: Biology
subject.description: Study of living organisms, their structure, function, and interactions within ecosystems.

topics.name: Mission 1: Osmosis – Water Transport Across Membranes
topics.short_description: Mechanism of water movement across semi-permeable membranes

content.text: Why do skin cells wrinkle in prolonged water exposure? This is due to osmotic water movement causing cell volume changes, optimizing grip function in wet conditions.

Water molecules move from regions of high water potential to low water potential across semi-permeable membranes. This process occurs naturally to equalize solute concentrations on either side of the membrane.
Osmosis is the passive diffusion of water molecules through a semi-permeable membrane, critical for maintaining turgor pressure in plant cells and homeostasis in animal cells.
H2L: Water moves from high to low concentration. Visualize it as water 'flowing downhill' through a membrane.
Maintains turgor in plant cells, preventing wilting, and regulates hydration and electrolyte balance in animal cells.
In hypertonic solutions, plant cells lose water and plasmolyze; in hypotonic solutions, cells gain water and become turgid. Practical experiments demonstrate osmotic principles.
Predict the response of a plant cell in pure water versus saline solution using osmotic principles.

topics.name: Mission 2: Photosynthesis – Solar Energy Conversion
topics.short_description: Biochemical process by which plants synthesize organic compounds from light energy

content.text: Plants synthesize glucose from carbon dioxide and water using light energy captured by chlorophyll.
Light-dependent reactions convert solar energy into chemical energy (ATP, NADPH); light-independent reactions fix CO2 into glucose via the Calvin cycle.
Photosynthesis equation: 6CO2 + 6H2O + light energy → C6H12O6 + 6O2
6-6 makes 1-6: 6 CO2 + 6 H2O → 1 Glucose + 6 O2
Occurs in chloroplasts of plant cells.
Without sunlight, photosynthesis ceases, halting glucose production and oxygen release.
photosynthesis.png

topics.name: Mission 3: Cellular Respiration – Energy Extraction
topics.short_description: Process by which cells convert glucose into usable chemical energy (ATP)

content.text: Cells metabolize glucose to produce ATP, which fuels cellular functions.
Glucose is oxidized in the presence of oxygen within mitochondria, releasing energy, carbon dioxide, and water.
Cellular respiration equation: C6H12O6 + 6O2 → 6CO2 + 6H2O + ATP
Respiration is the biochemical opposite of photosynthesis.
Occurs in mitochondria, the cell’s energy organelles.
During intense activity, increased respiration rate supplies additional ATP, explaining rapid breathing in athletes.
respiration.png
